title: Petrified Mass Production Eva before Air
display_title: Petrified Mass Production Eva before Air
wikitext:
[]: Petrified Mass Production Eva before Air?             Quote Post by visionfactory » Tue Jun 22, 2010 8:48 pm Hi, I'm new to the forum I had this question all my life I hope somebody here can help me :/ Why there is a petrified Eva Mass Production in the episode in which Shinji meets Kaworu? The 'stone' where Kaworu is sit at the beach.. Then the same 'stone' appears at the beggining of Rebirth.. and Rebirth happens before EoE so why there is that stone there? It really looks like a Eva Mass I made some screencaps.. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Tue Jun 22, 2010 8:54 pm We don't really know much other than it's a statue that looks a lot like an MP Eva. Here is some information you may find helpful.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 22, 2010 9:24 pm That statue Kaworu is sitting on is an angel (hah) statue.

[]:             Quote Post by visionfactory » Tue Jun 22, 2010 10:16 pm Thanks! The evacommentary article was really useful

[]:             Quote Post by Dark doom » Wed Jun 23, 2010 11:15 am It seam's that the MP EVA's have some kind of control over the time continuem.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 02 » Wed Jun 23, 2010 1:12 pm Haha funny, I've wondered the same thing since I saw the 24th episode many many years ago. I thought either 1) They (Seele) designed the Eva's according to the statue, which is of an angel warrior or something. 2.) They (creator[s]) designed the statues first, then when having to come up with a design for the Mass Produces, they thought hat would be a cool design to incorperate. Since it is headless, it could positively account for the conflict of how to make something with a long neck look cool. Try it out right now, just draw the statue and try to design a cool head on it right now. Its hard. So the Harpies' not having a cool head design could definitely be explained by this theory. It is also solidified when you call those collective statues "the final messangers from a theistic approach, then the MPE as the embodyment of that idolization.

[]:             Quote Post by CrazedSlayer » Wed Jun 23, 2010 4:48 pm I had personally never considered a link between the statue and the MP Evas, I thought it was more for symbolism of Kaworu's nature and the whole 'headlessness' thing being a grim reminder in EoE for what Shinji had to do. Ive never thought about a link to MP Eva's before, so I dont know how they could be arguably connected through a time-distortion theory, but it definitely sounds like an interesting topic for debate.

[]:             Quote Post by Aozora » Thu Jun 24, 2010 11:29 pm ...Interesting! I never looked closely at the statue before. That long neck is definitely suspicious. There are always new things in Eva that I never noticed or thought about before. I want to say stuff about Rebuild now, but I don't think that's allowed.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Jun 24, 2010 11:37 pm Eva 02 wrote: So the Harpies' not having a cool head design could definitely be explained by this theory.

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Fri Jun 25, 2010 4:29 pm The link ZapX provided is pretty much spot-on. I took it as foreshadowing personally come the second viewing. It was all wonderfully executed, there's a lot of great examples of foreshadowing throughout Eva but this would be my favourite actually [Along with the surreal imagery of multiple Rei's during her monologue were she repeats, "What am I?" Over and over] Though, it's hilarious that, without the Lilith connotation to example the angel's breasts you'd think it was saying Kaworu was a girl [Ok, I am not going to go there!]

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 02 » Fri Jun 25, 2010 4:50 pm Monk Ed wrote: Say wha-? Their heads were awesome. Anything I want to punch that badly is earning its 'bad guy' paycheck.

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Fri Jun 25, 2010 4:58 pm Eva 02 wrote: They're endearing because theyre evas, but the head design could be more badass.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 02 » Fri Jun 25, 2010 5:13 pm ^ Ok... I'm a man but I have no reservations in saying those things are fucking cute. I want them all.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Jun 25, 2010 5:50 pm I never noticed the Evaesque "stripes" on the statue's arms before. Yet the stripes are the only part of the statue hinting at an Evangelion. How bizarre.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Jun 25, 2010 7:49 pm visionfactory wrote: The evacommentary article was really useful

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jun 26, 2010 7:49 pm Thanks for posting my old article so early in the thread, Zap. Makes me feel all warm and fuzzy! For those wondering about the statue's design, note that the statue was redesigned, from its original appearance in #24 OA, for the end of DEATH. Also note that the NGE movies and the revised versions of #21~#24 were being produced together (with them ultimately being released in the order of: D&R, EoE, then finally the extended video versions of 21~24). Both the redesigned statue and the MP Evas debuted in the same film ( Death & Rebirth ). Since the "Rebirth" part was originally intended to be what we got with End of Evangelion : EoE Movie Book wrote: The remade episodes 25' and 26' were originally intended for release as OVA, but during production it was decided that they would be released in theaters. This was "EVANGELION DEATH AND REBIRTH" (Shin Seiki Evangelion Gekijouhen -- Shito Shinsei) which consists of two parts: "DEATH" which a summary of the TV series and the newly remade "REBIRTH" which was to serve as a climax. However, the content of the remake grew much larger than planned, so the content shown in "REBIRTH" was only about 2/3 of the new episode 25'. Thus, "EVANGELION DEATH and REBIRTH" was released in March 1997 as Part 1 of the conclusion.

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Sat Jun 26, 2010 9:46 pm Reichu wrote: ...putting prominent breasts on a statue that's first and foremost identified with Kaworu is intended to say something about Kaworu himself . That is, allude to part of his true nature, as the maternal being Adam -- breasts being classical symbols of Motherhood and Fertility.
